title: Fire Prevention Information - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Disaster Services > Fire Prevention Information 6 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF AMERICAN RED CROSS CASCADES REGION NORTHWEST OREGON CHAPTER SOUND THE ALARM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides education on home fire safety and the installation of fire alarms. A trained Red Cross volunteer will install a smoke alarm in the house, review a fire safety and home hazard checklist, and provide information about creating a fire escape plan. Also offers specialized bedside alarms for individuals who are deaf or hard-of-hearing. 3131 N Vancouver Avenue Portland, OR 97227 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 528-5783 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF ALBANY SAFE AND SECURE SENIORS INDEPENDENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides adults living independently a way to maintain safe and secure homes with security and fire safety inspections and education, installation of security hardware, work to make house addresses visible, engraving valuables, smoke alarm inspection, installation, and maintenance. Assistance is offered by volunteers and local law enforcement agencies. 333 Broadalbin Street SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 55 and older Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Home safety inspection: By appointment only Email (541) 917-7680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND FIRE & RESCUE ADMINISTRATION PORTLAND FIRE & RESCUE ADMINISTRATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Administrative headquarters for Portland Fire & Rescue. Provides public safety services including fire prevention, public education, response to fire, medical, and other emergency incidents, and disaster mitigation. Also issues fire permits. 55 SW Ash Street Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Administration: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Permits: Monday-Friday 8am-3:30pm Email (503) 823-3700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLIDE REVITALIZATION UMPQUA FLASH ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps reduce wildfire risk by visiting homes to assess fire hazards and educate residents. Provides tree trimming and vegetation clearing to create defensible space around homes. Also hosts community clean-up days to remove vegetation from private and public properties, and educational workshops on fire prevention and preparedness. 301 Glide Loop Drive Glide, OR 97443 Eligibility: Homeowners. (Renters may apply if the property owner approves the work and owner's signature is on program application.) Hours: April-May and October-December, subject to weather conditions Email (541) 671-0189 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON AND WASHINGTON WILDFIRE INFORMATION OREGON WILDFIRE RESPONSE AND RECOVERY WEBSITE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about current wildfires and air quality. Offers a resource directory for wildfire preparation, response, and recovery. Includes evacuation information and a sign-up for emergency alerts through OR-Alert. Provides a list of verified organizations collecting donations for people impacted by wildfire. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Visit Website More Details OREGON DEPARTMENT OF FORESTRY FIRE RESTRICTIONS AND CLOSURES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about public and industrial fire restrictions by county, burn bans, and resources for fire prevention. 2600 State Street Salem, OR 97310 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 945-7200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details AMERICAN RED CROSS CASCADES REGION SOUND THE ALARM CITY OF ALBANY SAFE AND SECURE SENIORS INDEPENDENT CITY OF PORTLAND FIRE & RESCUE ADMINISTRATION PORTLAND FIRE & RESCUE ADMINISTRATION GLIDE REVITALIZATION UMPQUA FLASH ASSISTANCE PROGRAM OREGON DEPARTMENT OF FORESTRY FIRE RESTRICTIONS AND CLOSURES
